decks.title: Architect Meeting Playbook
decks.description: Core framework for framing architecture discussions. Covers 6 problem domains with structured ask/default/say/avoid patterns.

decks.title: Domain Cheatsheet
decks.description: Quick reference for Azure HR/payroll/talent platform decisions across payroll, absence, onboarding, benefits, identity, and reporting.

decks.title: Meeting Response Pack
decks.description: Ready-made responses for common meeting scenarios including the 4-step pattern, situation-specific responses, and time-buying lines.

decks.title: Objection Handlers
decks.description: Responses to meeting clichés: single pane of glass, golden source, everything real-time, AI, Kubernetes, shared platform.

decks.title: Architecture Overreach
decks.description: Counters for architecture overreach: event sourcing, canonical models, one workflow engine, premature standardisation, and future-proofing.

scenarios.title: Platform Consolidation Meeting
scenarios.description: Navigate a VP-led meeting about consolidating five regional payroll engines into one platform. Handle scope creep, stakeholder translation, and architecture overreach.

scenarios.title: The Golden Source Debate
scenarios.description: Counter the push for a canonical data model across 14 systems. Separate operational consistency from reporting needs and establish domain ownership.

scenarios.title: The Real-Time Everything Request
scenarios.description: Decompose a sweeping 'make everything real-time' demand into right-sized solutions. Handle Kafka hype, competitive anxiety, and UX vs infrastructure confusion.

scenarios.title: The AI Pitch
scenarios.description: Respond to a CTO-driven push to add AI across the HR and payroll platform. Separate safe assistive AI from high-risk decision-making AI, handle GDPR exposure, and give concrete architectural guidance.

scenarios.title: One Workflow Engine to Rule Them All
scenarios.description: Push back on a platform mandate to standardise all orchestration on Logic Apps. Distinguish integration workflows, approval flows, domain orchestration, and case management — and negotiate a defensible carve-out.

scenarios.title: The Single Pane of Glass
scenarios.description: Reframe a new CPO's demand for a unified HR portal. Decompose 'single pane of glass' into login, navigation, search, and workflow problems — and present the micro-frontend alternative to a platform rebuild.

scenarios.title: The Golden Source Programme
scenarios.description: Respond to a CIO-sponsored 'One Employee Truth' programme proposing a central Employee Master service. Separate authoritative ownership by attribute and time model rather than accepting a single-write-path architecture.

scenarios.title: The Identity Boundary Debate
scenarios.description: Respond to CISO pressure to let HR own access decisions after a joiner/mover/leaver audit. Separate workforce facts from access policy enforcement and define the right integration between HR and IAM.

scenarios.title: The Reporting Truth Fight
scenarios.description: Address a CFO frustrated by conflicting headcount, payroll cost, and attrition numbers across teams. Establish that metric definitions and data ownership must be resolved before platform consolidation.

scenarios.title: The Shared Policy Hub
scenarios.description: Push back on a CTO proposal for an enterprise policy engine covering leave, overtime, compensation, and payroll rules. Separate configuration, decision tables, and domain-critical logic before committing to a shared platform.

scenarios.title: The Workflow Platform Mandate
scenarios.description: Frame a COO demand to standardise all employee lifecycle processes on one workflow platform. Classify orchestration types — integration flows, approvals, long-running sagas, and case management — before selecting tooling.

scenarios.title: The Switch That Must Not Surprise Anyone
scenarios.description: Guide an activation design review for the new Expenses module. Preserve clear entitlement boundaries, avoid Claims confusion, and prevent payroll side effects.

scenarios.title: A Warning Is Not a Decision
scenarios.description: Shape phase-one claim policy handling. Balance employee warnings, manager visibility, admin-configured automation, and tight scope boundaries.

scenarios.title: Read the Receipt, Not the Tea Leaves
scenarios.description: Review OCR receipt scanning decisions across UX, tax compliance, payroll mapping, and secure evidence handling.

scenarios.title: Ask the Policy, Not the Internet
scenarios.description: Design trustworthy in-flow AI policy guidance. Keep answers grounded in uploaded policy, include source traceability, and handle updates safely.

scenarios.title: The Miles, the Metrics, and the Money
scenarios.description: Align mileage reimbursement, analytics foundations, anomaly-readiness, and internal/external integration expectations.

scenarios.title: From Policy PDF to Enforced Rule
scenarios.description: Evaluate how policy text becomes enforceable configuration. Clarify rules engine sequencing, build-vs-buy decisions, and admin review controls.
